channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why do workers/poor hate investing? — 43 replies — 172.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do workers/poor hate investing?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:59 UTC

OP

Most conversation with friends/family/coworkers I have when it comes to investing long term in Roth IRA, high yield savings, and my ETF Dividend portfolio even with all the money I make working cyber security utilizing FIRE method they get so aggressive about “what if” scenarios thinking I’m day-trading and even when I explain it they don’t get it.

“WHAT IF THE MARKET ALL GOES UNDER AND YOU LOSE EVERYTHING”

“WHAT IF YOU TAKE ALL YOUR MONEY OUT AND SELL ON A LOSS”

“WHAT IF SOMETHING BAD HAPPENS AND YOU HAVE NO MONEY”

Which I retort what happens when you lose your job? When the market goes under and suddenly you’re up for layoffs?

“WELL THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN BECAUSE IM VALUABLE AND MY SKILLS AND MY TIME AR COMPANY AND-“

Like do these people really think working for someone for the rest of your life is the only way to live? I’d rather live frugally but free, and that really seems to irk people.


What the hell is this psychology? Why do they hate the idea someone doesn’t wanna work for someone for the rest of their life or majority? Do we forget money is to buy our freedom?

Early replies (first 3)

62294872
They are physically, mentally incapable of visualizing what you are trying to explain in terms of long term time horizons, compounding returns, etc. It's not worth bothering with. Like trying to talk to a dog.

62294877
There's a reason poor "people" are poor
The final redpill and the toughest to swallow is that jews are 100% right about the goyim

62294881
>>62294870
You probably come across as obnoxious and irritating and you're clearly in your early 20s and lack the maturity to keep shit to yourself. People don't just randomly get aggressive the way you've described, you're missing the key details and you're probably rubbing people up the wrong way and saying all the wrong this because you lack tact and grace.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294912
>>62294904
having tact and grace means conforming to their standard of being nothing more than carbon based slaves (that AI will replace anyways)

62294913
>>62294905
You are the one that made a post on 4chan complaining about your interactions with wageslaves and mentally shadowboxing arguments that didn't happen. If you're actually 30 years old then thats just sad, most people grow out of this 4chan angstposting when they're like 19, so no matter how much money you have you're still a manchild.

62294916
>>62294904
Correct. Even if you are right, only manchildren and intellectually disabled spergs go about shouting about it.

62294919
>>62294913

This dude is insisting OP must have done something incendiary to set someone off in a financial discussion and he himself went off the rails ass blasted based off of nothing but the suggestion that he doesn't understand money lmfao

62294920
>>62294913
yeah well said
Normal well adjusted 30 year olds should be poor wage slaves like you!

62294921
>>62294916
I don't think he was shouting about it and telling wagies "you're fucking stupid for not saving". I think he was trying to connect with them about finance, you know like how they connect about meaningless bullshit like media consumption, and it hurts them because he's getting ahead and their lives are going nowhere.

62294923
>>62294919
Nobody is assblasted, little boy. Its very obvious OP (probably you on a different device btw) that the way this engagement came about and how it started and ended is missing about 90% of the detail, your miserable and retarded personality.

62294926
>>62294870
Normies have cars, houses and girlfriends
They invested well, for them

62294927
>>62294913
Well hey, I’m happy how I am buddy and enjoying my life.

Just wanted to ask if anyone understands the psychology and hear their experiences making conversation.

You shouldn’t let others conversations bother you anon. Coming to posts to be derogatory and aggressive to strangers online is quite immature. Take your own advice if this is the case hun. I don’t gotta conform to your ideas and beliefs of how I should act at my age when I’m living and thriving. Not to mention fulfilled and happy.

Stop projecting anon. I just want my friends to experience the same.

62294929
>>62294926
they could have all of that and not having to be a wageslave when they're 40

62294932
>>62294921
Most normal people upon hearing about investing and alternative ways of accumulating wealth, do not launch in to this interrogation that OP wrote fanfiction about. He's missing out the key details, and I suspect the way he got himself in to this was the way he conveyed it and is probably a sperg. Just telling the truth

62294934
>>62294926
You know what? If they enjoy their life good for them. But they sure bitch a lot about it. I’ve never heard a rich person who saved or came from old money bitch about the market yelling at them or threatening to fire them. Just only when it goes under.

But if they’re happy with those complaints? Good for them. You’re right anon.

62294935
>>62294927
You are coming across as a very sensitive and spergy young man. You posted on /biz/ begging for reassurance and to "laugh at le normies" because someone challenged you on your retarded lack of social grace and you clearly got very offended by it.

I'm just telling you that 4chan isn't going to reinforce and support your narcissistic personality disorder. Nobody said anything about happiness, you are projecting now. You are a 30 year old man that is so thin-skinned, you needed this aging millennial japanese imageboard to suck you off when you got bitchslapped by reality. Pathetic

62294943
>>62294920
Are you ESL? Did you understand my post? Do you have autism?

62294946
>>62294935
You’re not very experienced at trolling my man you gave away your position after 3 posts at this point lol

If you’re actually this pressed, please stop projecting anon.

62294958
>>62294946
Nobody here is pressed besides you. You got boxed in to a corner by a based boomer and you ran back to your incelfort and wrote screed seething about the encounter. Making fun of you for this behaviour I guess could be considered trolling but I'm just picking up what you voluntarily put down.

62294980
>>62294958
Good boy, you got your attention now hostily because your friends and family were never nice to you growing up.

Do you feel better? Do you like having hostile eyes upon you because nice ones feel uncomfortable because they never happened?

I’m sorry anon. I hope this fulfills you in some way besides short term. That or I hope you find success.

62294993
>>62294980
I haven't talked about myself this whole thread. Are you a woman by any chance? I cannot imagine a 30 year old man typing like this. This only supports the argument I'm making that you have zero social skills and clearly come in to a lot of friction and conflict with people who aren't terminally online, and you use 4chan as a kind of a imaginary friend reinforcement of your retardation and mental illness. Whats up with those normies amirite guys?

62294999
>>62294980
Just take the L and walk away. The more you respond, the more you make a fool of yourself.

62295006
>>62294932
Agreed. OP probably called his mom a wagie goycuck when she told him that dumping all his money into the market and trying to retire at 30 was retarded. He then went on 4chan to look for validation and is now crashing out in the thread because people here are calling him retarded too.

62295029
>>62294932
>missing out the key details
you're brown

62295032
I lost over 200k on crapcoins

62295046
>>62295029
Nah its more like:

>hi buddy you doing good? had a tough week down at the plant haha makes you wonder why anybody does this amirite haha

And his response was:

>err you know you could just invest in Roth IRA, high yield savings, and diversify your ETF Dividend portfolio utilizing FIRE method? Sounds like you've taken the bluepill like a poor little goycuck goycattle betamale

62295060
>>62294870
Status quo bias

62295086
I don’t interact with poor people much but my experience is that they get upset if they hear that they actually have agency. that there is a way out through their own actions feels like an attack on them. easier to think you’re just a victim of circumstance. so if you say I’ve done x, y, or z, they think you’re saying you’re better than them. and in a way you are saying that and you are in fact better than them. the poor are superstitious in a way that reduces the role of their own agency. if you do well, you are merely lucky. if you do badly, it has to do with fate, not your own actions.

62295139
I side with OP, poor people are irrational.

If you're 100% honest with poor working class people they will hate you.
You have to lie and say you have a job, a mortgage, problems, etc. because they don't want to hear about you winning.

Just get your money and live your life and be careful how you present yourself around poors they're like a different caste of lower human.

62295155
It's the inability to think further ahead than just the next month or year.
My Father has been showing me newspaper clippings of "GOLD PRICES SOAR HIGHER THAN EVER!" for at least the last 15 years, with the usual comment of "I should have bought Gold years ago!". We have this conversation every year, and I tell him that NOW is the time to buy, but what does he say? "No, it's so high right now, I'm sure it can't go any higher. You can only lose if you buy now!"

62295158
>>62294980
>seething this hard
You lost and lost hard, you eternal child. Go cry someplace that cares.

62295173
>>62295155
I recently saw a YouTube video where a guy showed what it would look like if you invested $1,000 every month into the S&P500 if you bought at the absolute worst time (highest price) for each month for seven years. It still made a ton of money. Even if you had the worst luck in human history and chose the worst day to invest every single time you still come out ahead.

62295177
>>62294881

>Bro i know you barely make ends meet already, but if you put 90% of your income into #FARTPEPE you'll be rich bro! I made a killing on it using mommy's creditcard that one time. Why are you so angry bro, don't you want to be a smart investor like me? Why do you hate investing? Why are you subhuman worker trash incapable of understanding simple financial advice? I'm just trying to help!

item.title: Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we… — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:56 UTC

OP

Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your weekend is already spiritually finished. What's that? You didn't come any closer to finding a way out of wagie hell this weekend? OOPS, I guess you'll have to try again next weekend HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62293547
wdym depressed
if im not at work i literally dont know what do do with myself, i just shitpost on /smg/ while waiting for the market to open
I wish I could work 7 days a week, more money I could throw in the market

62293578
>>62293533
I don't even know who these threads are for. Are there /biz/ who work at amazon warehouses? I feel like that would be rare.

62293839
>>62293533
This has to be the real Bezos. Who else could be this consistent?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293843
>>62293533
you're such an asshole lol

62293898
>>62293533
Well at least I still have both my parents. You don't have your mother who u cried like a bitch at her death after being a dementia vegetable for so long. While she was alive she gazed at you with animal like uncomprehending eyes and u had to gaze back and realize that mom u knew literally doesn't exist

62293907
>>62293533
your rocket ejaculated too early now cleanup that mess Jeffy

62293911
finally we have an answer to him

62293954
>>62293908
Paid for by wagies

62293966
>>62293954
in the end, everything is paid for by wagies, comrade
but in the immediate reckoning, this is on bezos 100%. on his company to be precise, which is a large fraction of his nw
wagies still get their wages even if the rocket goes boom

62293968
>>62293533
at least im not married to some bimbo spic slut

62293969
>>62293533
Saturday to recover
Sunday to prepare
A wagie's weekend vanishes into thin air!

62293976
plz stop mr. bezos

62293981
Every time a bezos thread is posted his start flexing that u have a mother who is alive and doesn't have dementia

62294021
>>62293908
KEK oh nononono bezosissies....

62294023
>>62293533
Was a nice four day week last week wasn't it, wagie. Now you're staring down the barrel of a fresh five

62294025
>>62293908
You will have the incident investigation finalised and on my desk Monday morning sharp, won't you wagie.

62294069
>>62293533
I swear its the real Bezos posting these

62294070
>>62293533
Jokes on you, I'm prepping my resignation letter.

62294254
>>62294069
What else would you do with his wealth? It gives you unlimited power to humiliate wagies.

62294795
>>62293533
It's ok, next week is another week. I will flip so many burgers, I swear. Only 10000 more years of saving 100k a year until I become a billionaire like you, master.

The hopium is real.

62295016
>>62293908
clean it up, wagie
>>62293954
>Paid for by wagies
this is how the jew make his money

62295035
>>62293839
Possibly, in any case he is right

62295099
>>62293578
I've always wondered about this. Also most wages don't get the whole weekend off, they usually get a random day off in the week

62295165
I love the pure seethe that originates as a result of this sentiment. Wagies beg for opportunities to be made slaves and then get upset at the realization that they are nothing but. This system is the greatest in the world and the mass of tireless goyim work wonderfully. Another week off for me!

item.title: When are PC parts going to be affordable again? — 36 replies — 84.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
When are PC parts going to be affordable again?

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:35 UTC

OP

When are PC parts going to be affordable again? I'm tired of not being able to play games made in this decade.

Early replies (first 3)

62294333
>>62294325
Stop playing games you child

62294453
>>62294325
Not sure but Macbook Neo seems reasonably priced to me.

62294457
>>62294333
This. You should be focusing on activities that will lift you up from not being able to afford PC parts.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294553
>>62294325
a fully prebuilt 5090 pc is like 6k still, if you could not afford it before, you still cant afford it now
nothing has changed, problem is you are being too poor
if you had more money like anons said this would not even be a problem

keep in mind you would keep this pc for the next 3 years or until the connector burns up because nv release cycles are now longer so 2k a year at worst, if you wanna stay on the bleeding edge, you cant afford 2k a year on pc? get a job nikker

>>62294457
>>62294531
>>62294539
truez

62294556
>>62294325
I’m playing the game with all the computer parts for the masses

62294586
>>62294539
>If you bought the right lottery ticket you'd be rich

62294598
>>62294586
ram prices started to increase exponentially back in October, you literally had 4 months to see this coming

62294638
>>62294598
you had 10 years to predict the AI boom

62294654
>>62294638
I believed in my ram predictions, did you believe in your ai predictions

62294665
>>62294654
I invested in amd and nvidia in 2019 so, yeah.

62294669
>>62294325
If you haven't bought spare gaming pcs after seeing what happened to NIVIDIA GPUs, it's honestly on you.
I saw the writing in the wall and bought a cheap gaming pc with an AMD GPU for $700 on black friday and just held on to it just in case my current one breaks so I probably have 10-20 years before having to get a new one.

62294681
>>62294665
ok so we have the same opinion that this retard should have invested?

62294719
>Might have to save up for an extra another couple of months for gayming machine
Aww diddums

62294724
How old are you people?
Is this board filled with teens?
fuck your video games, grow up you useless fat incel faggot

62294742
>>62294453

I was watching a raid array rebuild and zening out and it hit me, normies like you, apple marketing shill spammers, you will never be able to have an offline LAN

You are too stupid and lazy. It's simply beyond you.The reasons why you would need one are incomprehensibe to someone who thinks brands are status symbols. The fact that LAN cables don't have a brand that signifies status to fools means you can't even understand why LAN cables exist or who would want one. I mean you know you are lying and selling awful underpowered overpriced 8gb uninspired thrash that traps consumers into an awful corporate walled garden cloud portal devices hell but you are so lacking ability, characater, morals, technical ability you are willing to spam this junk for low wages. That's your life. You probably spend most of it on food and rent and then use the free apple homosexual mating call product you get to sit in starbucks trying to hit on other men, hoping some elderly faggot will adopt you and you can live with them as a carer/prositute in exchange for some near geriatric ejaculating in your anus. Even if you won a lottery you would spend it all on overpriced, poor value brandslop logos and subscriptions, the little brain you have was completely overpowered by modern marketing, so much so you ended up a minion of it.

You're the perfect example of the future digital slave, you will have to subscribe to get access to remote storage, cloud games, music, movies and computing resources accessed via terrible tiny screened, overpriced devices with awful specs deliberately designed to be useless for anything other that cloud access portals. Yet you will proudly display your slave brand logo as if telling the world you are a good slave before you go and let some geriatric ejaculate in your anus for food and then spend any time you have left spamming apple marketing idiocy in desperation to get another free sample because it means everything to you to own gank brandslop

62294743
>>62294333
I refuse there people over the age of 21 who still play video games who aren't influencers paid to do it

62294746
>>62294681
yeah, only normalfags didn't find it obvious
we had pimeyes in 2019 and gpt in 2020

62294758
>>62294742
Least schizophrenic LAN shill

62294762
>>62294743
>>62294724
>I'm a big boy, I don't need toys anymore
You're stuck at the mental age of a 14 year old. Vidya is for truly mature people.

62294794
>>62294325
They won't be until nearly 2028 at earliest but the problem then will be that a lot of the companies that support pc building like case maers, fan, makers, psu makers, thermal paste makers etc will be gone. Buy what is cheap now, get your best case, a really really good high wattage PSU, thermal paste, fans etc and hold your money for one- two years for the rest.
>>62294535
>My desktop has been gathering dust for the past 7-8 years. I just use an old laptop for work and music and if I want to play a game I get my 360 or ps2.
I play a lot of XP era games on an XP retro build. Based onn the Ps2 and 360, I do the same but for most it is too late to get a library and they are collectors items now.

62294799
>>62294453
I genuinely see baffling shit like this on /biz/ daily. I'm starting to think being rich makes you retarded.

62294806
>>62294758
Offlinne LANs are the future anon. We both know it.

62294823
>>62294325
The plan is for you to never be able to outright buy your own parts and computer but rather rent the service to play

62294842
>>62294762
There are real bers and zoomer faux nerds. Which one are you? It's the 21 century a man has to have options, money, pussy, drugs, vidya, literature, art, music, film, gardening, hunting, gemology, riding, carpentry, electronics repair., shooting, archery, antqie restoration an collecting, stamp collecting, killing,electronics, programming, driving, motorbikes, falconry, dogs, fitness, martial arts, guitar, piano, harmonica, mandolin, bird spotting, model trains, lego, cooking, writing, academics, casting lea soliders and bulets, sculupture, painting, jewlery making, fine dining, metal work, hiking, military history, tabletop games, photography, war games, chess, thrifting, reading, climbing, tennis, squash, singing, male choir,. Knife sharpening, Making computers, glass blowing, fighting, electronic repair, inciting wars, political philosophy, concert attendence, tracking and killing enemies of your nation, horology, explosives, smallholding, forestry, done racing, learning languages, model aircraft, ham radio, trading crypto stocks and bonds etc etc.

Playing games is just one of those options. They are all fine hobbies, none better that another

62294909
>>62294325
>When are PC parts going to be affordable again?

When openAI goes bankrupt.

62294947
>>62294842
Pretty based, I do most of them. I'd incite wars but I lack the funds. But normalfags (who are female minded) dismiss any hobby that doesn't get them laid and consider the worship of pussy to be the deepest thing in life, bar none. They want to be awash in gash. Women avoid introverted hobbies like the AIDS. Women like money, socializing, displays of psychopathy, extraversion, low IQ, physicality, etc.

62294961
>>62294724
gb2 reddit normalfag

62294979
>>62294947

Man, this argument falls apart the second you stop treating women like a hive mind.

You're basically taking a few traits you've noticed in some people and stretching them across billions of individuals. That's not insight; that's stereotyping with extra steps. There are tons of women who spend their free time reading, gaming, drawing, writing, coding, collecting random niche interests, or disappearing into obscure rabbit holes for hours. The idea that women avoid introverted hobbies "like AIDS" is just obviously false to anyone who's spent five minutes online.

The money and psychopath thing isn't much stronger. If women were only attracted to wealth, status, and flashy antisocial behavior, then shy guys, nerds, artists, academics, and regular dudes would never date. Yet they do, every day. Reality keeps refusing to cooperate with the theory.

And the "normalfags are female-minded" line is weird too. Most people care about relationships because relationships are a major part of human life. That's true for men and women. Having goals outside of niche hobbies doesn't mean someone worships sex. It just means they have different priorities.

The claim that women prefer low IQ is probably the funniest part. In the real world, competence, intelligence, and capability are generally attractive traits. Not universally, because humans are complicated, but far more often than the caricature you're describing.

The biggest problem with this worldview is that it tries to reduce millions of different people into one cynical template. Some women are introverts. Some are extroverts. Some care about money a lot. Some barely care at all. Some like athletes. Some like engineers. Some like artists. Human beings are way too varied to fit into a single edgy slogan.

At some point the theory starts looking less like an observation about women and more like a story you're telling yourself about them.

62294991
>>62294325
PC parts have always been affordable but waitfags expect new GPUs to cost $30

62294992
>>62294325
"Cheap" computing was a market anomaly and we're just returning to normal. Maybe wait 5-15 years and we could see that paradigm return.

62294994
>>62294979

62295011
>>62294992
>market anomaly
Only because despite being cheap to manufacture now, there's absolutely no incentive for the elite to give normalfags fully open hardware and software. The future is fully locked down crap like Androids and iOS. Neutered for "your" safety, of course.

62295101
>>62294961
we use mainchan around here

item.title: SATA bought over 2k BTC this week — 16 replies — 57.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
SATA bought over 2k BTC this week

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:44 UTC

OP

The new investment offering 13% in daily dividends bought 2k BTC this week.

Strive the company behind SATA only owns 15k BTC so far so this is huge.

It's marked to have another huge day today probably going to finish around another 500 BTC bought.

Early replies (first 3)

62288571
wow 2K btc

to the moon

62288604
>>62288571
They grew their BTC treasury by 10% in one week.
2k BTC is more than El Salvador bought in the last 2 years.

This is rocket fuel because everyone is getting 13% and BTC is getting slurped up.
This will send the price to 250k in a year.

62290655
Holy Bitcoin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291854
>>62288531
>13% APR paid daily
We understand that this is unsustainable, right? This is an obscene amount to be paying out, it cannot last forever at this percentage. What's their exit strategy? Is there any?

62292377
>>62291854
It's 13% for the year, if you auto buy more with the dividend it's 13.88% the dividend pays everyday.

They're strategy is that Bitcoin goes up 30%+ a year and they make loads of money.

They're chief risk officer interviewed coffeezilla and destroyed him in a debate, it's legit.

62292430
>>62290655
So they literally just copied Saylors' Ponzi scheme but threw an extra 3% payout on top to make sure it collapses faster.

62292449
>>62288531
Oh boy oh boy, if these schemes don't collapse in the next 2 years, it might literally the catalyst of next bear market bottom.

Saylor's migrated some bitcoin to Coinbase and Nakamoto Inc. selling and reporting huge losses.

I hope these niggers in fancy suits are taking into account that btc will at highest reach 200k next bull

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/bitcoin-firm-nakamotos-stock-hits-150928904.html

62292487
>>62292377
I like Jeff Walton. I like Michael Saylor. I don't own mstr or asst or any of the preferreds. I want to see how the market reacts when a bitcoin bull market kicks off. I know they'll be able to issue the common stock as mnav goes up. My curiosity is what happens to the preferred shares? I know it's a variable rate and it will inevitably force a raise to keep it at par but will that be enough or will they have to buyback the shares as well? So now instead of using funds to increase the bitcoin exposure, they're forced to deleverage. Then to make the common stock attractive again, they either issue debt to lever up the balance sheet or sell bitcoin to buyback the common stock. It's not a ponzi but it's extremely cyclical unless bitcoin does just go up only but it still seems extremely cyclical as well. Definitely interesting but I'd rather just hold bitcoin.

62292499
>>62292449
if they can remain solvent until 2029 they won

62292640
>>62288531
Fuck that theyre run by that poojeet vivek ramaswamy. All bizraelies should buy microstrategy and support the white man. Not to mention poojeets history of scamming people with his old biotech company

62294034
>>62292449
I guess that's the problem with treasury plays. They can fucking sell it and you end up with a trash stock that doesn't even have the bitcoin you bought it for.

62294403
>>62288531
7k ASST shares here. Already 20% up. Gonna hold till at least Btc is 300k.
If btc falls to 60k im buying OTM leaps for ASST 2028. Leverage on top of free leverage, fucking beauty

62294637
Isn't SATA that airdrop from like 5 or 7 years ago we all made bank on and then it disappeared?
Some Aussie?
Did he make it?

62295109
>>62288531
Bitcoin treasures are the future of the space imo

62295121
>>62295109
>treasuries

62295133
>>62294403
They have to dilute shareholders. Don't go too deep otm. Stay close to the money.

item.title: lever up on XLM now. — 30 replies — 51.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
lever up on XLM now.

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:25 UTC

OP

this pile of shit is always good for the yearly crime candle and it's due with the DTCC news.
>Only coin in the top 100 green today
>Massive spike in volume on spot and perps (2nd day)
They'll crime this one for a at least a few more days. Time to make your money back anon. Ogs remember the 2021 xlm crime candle that kickstarted the bull cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62283130
buncha noobs also going to short it cus muh mean reversion everything else is crashing!

62283353
>>62283344

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283356
>>62283117
What is a crime candle?

62283608
>>62283356
It's a politically correct alternative term.

62283617
>>62283356

62287293
>>62283617
That's a bart nig

62287816
https://x.com/Dr_Kritsada/status/2060289369047798078?s=20

62287833
So fxcking bullish xlm

62291090
posted at 17c btw, 5x long to 26c woulda been over a fast 2.5x

62291186
>>62291090
>just 5x long a vertical chart in a bear market bro
lol piss off

62291237
>>62291186
that's what made it so easy, 99.8% of the coins were dying that day and XLM was glowing green with huge volume, you don't fade that type of strength. XLM is a known crime coin, when it moves it moves big for at least a few days. It wasn't just gonna be a 1 day pnd.

62291244
clowns trying to short it cause muh too green in bear market is simply what propels it higher. I was watching the movement on binance perps they were luring people in baiting shorts but maintaining the overall structure before they'd rip it higher.

62291389
>>62291244
Fair play you crazy cat but it wasn't an easy call

62291531
>>62283117
So is this pretty much confirmation that chainlink doesn’t direct value to their token if the same news that made link pump 0% makes this shit coin pump 50%+

62291534
bullish asfxck at xlm

62291537
Holy shit my XLM bags are going to the moon

Imagine being a chainlinkie right now and being left out top kek

62291630
>>62291537
This

62291702
>>62291630
checked

62291715
>>62291531
Its the opposite

XLM is useless shitcoin so they arent suppressing it to accumulate more

62291718
>>62291715
>suppressing it to accumulate more

SAAAAAAAAAAAAAAR

62291732
>>62291718
t. rajeesh

62291857
>>62291732
saaar they are surpressing the link price so they can accumulate more link saaaar whales are buying do not fall for the fud

62294207
its dumping. kek baggies.

62294514
>>62294207
>Crypto only goes up

62295062
>>62283117
i love you retards.

62295069
>>62294481

item.title: Im in my 30's, and already dramatically surpassed my parents net worth by at least 10X despite them being in their late… — 5 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Im in my 30's, and already dramatically surpassed my parents net worth by at least 10X despite them being in their late…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-31 14:39 UTC

OP

Im in my 30's, and already dramatically surpassed my parents net worth by at least 10X despite them being in their late 60's.

They never invested in their life and believe investing is a scam to steal your money. So all they've done is either spend or save their fiat, and did nothing else with it.

Even today, when they see what I've achieved, they consider it purely "Luck" and nothing else, and that investing is still a scam, ie: That I am one of the lucky ones.


FYI: Yes they understand inflation and how their dollars lose value month to month, but they dont care, they just accept that as the norm, and that trying to avoid it, you risk losing what little you have.

Early replies (first 3)

62295048
>>62295040
it’s odd that understand inflation but not investing. it’s usually the other way around

62295051
Yeah, every single person who invested in the stock market in the past 80 years and held for any considerable length of time just got lucky. They call it the 80 year luck streak.

62295089
>>62295048
This. OPs parents probably saw a lot of downturns (dot com bubble, housing crash, COVID, etc) and OP, since he's in his 30s, has only been investing in the last decade which has had an amazing bull run.
The real test is to show OP a decade of downturn like (2000–2009) where the market finished the decade lower than where it started, and see if he changes his tune.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62295100
Do you have a wife and kids?

62295118
>>62295048
They fear massive draw downs on investing to the point where they dont want to ever do anything.

In early Jan 2017 when bitcoin hit $1k I remember telling my parents, if you just buy here, you can sell at the previous all time high and make out with 40%+ .

My mom thought about it, but didnt want to go ahead with it. Even now despite seeing where Bitcoin went, she still is bearish on it, thinking, it could go to zero at anytime.

>>62295100
I'm 5'7 in a modern dating market, so no, of course not.

Though I am thinking I should just find some women and come up with some kind of mutual benefit situation where I'll pay her to LARP as a mother and have my kids and raise them. But IDK what else to do other than that at this point.

>gets CIA agent job
>requests 666.6 pounds in gold bars for “work related expenses”
Everyone I know who is great at getting jobs is a lying thief. These people have no problem shaking your head while looking you in the eyes and lie to you but they always get hired quickly

62291831
>>62291829
yeaaaah, i'm starting a new cage job this Monday. I don't wanna do it. also I'm getting a reply on fucking monday whether my consultancy gig is going through, so on fucking Monday while waging I know whether I shouldn't had even showed up.
>Grim

62291832
Its the King's Birthday on monday so i shant be working in the goy factory

62291837
>>62291832
Doom island, crack a beer for me cunt

62291846
>>62291829
LVHC_#44799272 of warehouse_US_3322, are you recharging for this weeks hard factory work? Good. Less then 48 hours remain. P.s That rocket launch wasnt a failure it was deliberate. The freshly severed LVHC needed to be disposed of. The severance package entailed a quick, fully paid departure if they boarded the rocket.

62291922
>>62291829
the rocket isn't gonna fix itself. Clean it up wagie and then back in your cagie!

62292383
What? I have a 12 hour shift in a kitchen today.

62292615
OP is a retarded faggot, this nigger will never stop bezos posting on this board

62293054
Worked in the wage cage for about a week when I was 19 and addicted to heroin, spent most of the day jerking off or playing runescape2. Good times

62293068
>>62291829
bezos is next

62294027
>>62291829
Scrabble is my favorite board game.

62294029
>>62291829

imagine making that a poster and putting on the wall of an amazon warehouse. lol do you thing the wagies will gain consciousness

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 148 replies — 37.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 148 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:00 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

62294591
>>62271960
They will say the same thing when it hit 200K Kek. Honestly BTC has a lot of potential most especially since only 1% is in Defi, Imagine how huge it will be with TBV now in play.

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 73 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-05-31 11:05 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 45 replies — 17.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-05-31 12:26 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293920
>>62293876
No no you're right. Like, it would have been epic if you cashed out on the low 7 figs.

And you do need courage, balls etc to take risk. You are correct.

But any miami cunt who comes at me trying to shill crypto will get crushed under my intellect.

If you're honest bout the game it's all good imo

62293926
>>62293876
also the stealth thing is pretty smart desu. lowers vectors of attack against you. status signalling does have some practical uses but you probably already are smart enough to know when and where to flaunt your status.

62293934
>>62293920
anyone who is spiritually miami deserved to be crushed, that is true.

i'm just some bloke who used tism pattern recognition and intuition to financially help himself and his family. i'm not sorry for any of it and i'd do it all again. i pay my tax like a good goy and don't have to wagie because of this, literally every day i'm free to do as i please. it was worth it desu. take risks in life. doesn't have to be crypto, fuck crypto if you hate it. just bet on yourself in some avenue of life. risk it all.

62294194
Chainlink solves multiple real world problems. If you're too dumb to see that, maybe lurk a little longer and read more.
More generally, cryptography, and the financial instruments being developed that are secured by it, strengthens capitalism. It gives more tools to more people and increases the efficiency of the economy. Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money. If you can't appreciate that, you should suck your double barrel that's on your nightstand and get the fuck off of my board you decrepit, balding abomination

>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money

62294326
